task_completion.description: Verify the Multiparameter Monitor is configured with a Heart Rate of approximately 110 bpm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device simulator window for a 'Multiparameter Monitor' showing a Heart Rate (HR) value between 105 and 115 bpm.

task_completion.description: Verify the Non-Invasive Blood Pressure (NIBP) device is configured with a Systolic Pressure of approximately 90 mmHg.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device simulator window for an 'NIBP' (Non-Invasive Blood Pressure) device showing a Systolic pressure value between 85 and 95 mmHg.

task_completion.description: Verify the Infusion Pump is configured with an Infusion Rate greater than 10 mL/hr.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an OpenICE device simulator window for an 'Infusion Pump' showing an infusion rate of 10 mL/hr or higher.

task_completion.description: Verify the Vital Signs clinical application is open and displaying the integrated data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenICE 'Vital Signs' clinical application window. It should be actively displaying the aggregated data (HR ~110, Systolic BP ~90) from the configured simulated devices.

task_completion.description: Verify the status note is written to the correct file on the Desktop with the required information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/shock_protocol_status.txt'. The text must confirm the shock protocol configuration and explicitly list the current HR and BP values.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the OpenICE graphical interface to configure the devices and view the Vital Signs app.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual OpenICE GUI windows for the device simulators and the Vital Signs application, rather than just terminal commands or mocked text outputs.

integrity.description: Ensure the values written in the status note match the actual configured values in the OpenICE applications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The HR and BP values typed into the 'shock_protocol_status.txt' file should exactly match the numbers visible on the OpenICE Vital Signs application or device simulators in the screenshots.

integrity.description: Ensure the Vital Signs application is genuinely receiving data from the simulators.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Vital Signs app should show the specific values configured in the individual device simulators, indicating successful interoperability, rather than default or empty values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenICE is a medical device interoperability platform. The task requires configuring three simulated devices: 1) Multiparameter Monitor with Heart Rate between 105-115 bpm. 2) NIBP (Non-Invasive Blood Pressure) device with Systolic BP between 85-95 mmHg. 3) Infusion Pump with a rate >= 10 mL/hr. The 'Vital Signs' clinical application must be open to display this integrated data. Finally, a text file at /home/ga/Desktop/shock_protocol_status.txt must exist, confirming the setup and listing the configured HR and BP values.
